data.title: VIAVI Highlights Importance of Trusted Partner Collaboration in AI Era and Outlines Core Solution Areas for Communications and Networking at MWC Barcelona
data.description:
Over 30 demonstrations across AI data center, security and quantum safe, mission-critical communications, AIOps, 6G and AI-RAN to be showcased at stand 5B18

CHANDLER, Ariz., Feb. 18, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- VIAVI Solutions Inc. ( VIAV ) has announced its demonstration lineup for Mobile World Congress (MWC) Barcelona 2026, which takes place March 2-5. At VIAVI's booth 5B18, VIAVI will showcase its latest network testing, automation and intelligence solutions, with technology demonstrations across security and quantum-safe communications, mission-critical communications, AIOps, the AI data center, and 6G / AI-RAN.

"Networks, AI, security, wireless, photonics and sensing have all previously been separate domains, but they are now converging into a single, tightly coupled system," said Sameh Yamany, Chief Technology Officer, VIAVI. "This convergence is redefining how networks and critical infrastructure must be designed, validated and protected in these AI-first environments. At MWC, we reflect this new reality. Testing is shifting from components to validating and optimizing behavior, trust and resilience at scale."

VIAVI will also host a live digital twin use case demonstration from the stand each day at 4 PM CET, which will highlight how all solution areas featured at the booth integrate into a complete end-to-end digital twin.

More than 30 demonstrations across five core solution areas will be featured at the VIAVI booth. These include deep dives into the digital twin technologies required to train AI-RAN algorithms for 6G, AI data center scale up / scale out validation, assured Position, Navigation and Timing (APNT), Non-Terrestrial Network (NTN) testing and performance verification, and optimization for PQC/QKD. VIAVI's new ePRTC360+™, the only non-Cesium holdover clock to maintain 100 ns accuracy in GNSS-denied environments, will also be on display.

As technology domains converge, partnerships become even more critical. In collaboration with more than 20 partner organizations, VIAVI will highlight additional demonstrations, including Agentic AI-RAN digital twins, ray-tracing-based lab testing of real-world UE behavior and ISAC applications for disaster monitoring. These collaborators include the AI-RAN Alliance, Amphenol, AWS, Calnex, Ericsson, GlobalLogic, Infosys, ITRI, Nokia, NVIDIA, R Systems, Rohde & Schwarz and more.

About VIAVI
VIAVI is a global leader in test and measurement and optical technologies. Our test, monitoring, assurance, and resilient position, navigation and timing solutions enable and secure critical infrastructure ranging from data center ecosystems and communication networks to military, aerospace, railway and first responder communications. In addition, we develop and advance technologies used in high-volume optical applications across anti-counterfeiting, consumer electronics, aerospace, industrial and automotive end markets.

Learn more about VIAVI at www.viavisolutions.com. Follow us on VIAVI Perspectives, LinkedIn and YouTube.

data.title: Research Alert: CFRA Maintains Hold Opinion On Shares Of Genuine Parts Company
data.description:
03:55 PM EST, 02/17/2026 (MT Newswires) -- CFRA, an independent research provider, has provided MT Newswires with the following research alert. Analysts at CFRA have summarized their opinion as follows:

We lower our 12-month target by $10 to $135, based on a 2027 P/E of 15.8x, a justified discount to GPC's 10-year mean forward P/E of 18.1x. We lower our adjusted EPS estimates to $7.80 from $8.45 for 2026 and to $8.55 from $9.10 for 2027. Following GPC's Q4 miss and disappointing 2026 EPS guidance, we lower our estimates and price target but maintain a Hold on the shares. In our view, the stock lacks a catalyst and we see better growth opportunities across the space. However, we think the announced separation of the automotive and industrial businesses makes sense given the very different end markets, although it is a move which might not come to fruition for another year or more (a disappointment to investors hoping for a faster closing). GPC's recent operational execution has been poor, as its earnings have now fallen short of consensus in four of the past seven quarters. As such, while we remain positive on auto aftermarket fundamentals, there are other pure-play names that we prefer in the space.

data.title: Research Alert: CFRA Cuts Opinion On Shares Of General Mills, Inc. To Hold From Buy
data.description:
03:55 PM EST, 02/17/2026 (MT Newswires) -- CFRA, an independent research provider, has provided MT Newswires with the following research alert. Analysts at CFRA have summarized their opinion as follows:

GIS cut its FY 26 (May) outlook ahead of today's CAGNY 2026 event. The company now expects organic net sales to decline 1.5%-2%, down from prior guidance of -1% to +1%, and adj. EPS to fall 16%-20% in constant currency, vs the prior -10% to -15%. Management cited historically low consumer sentiment due to the cumulative impact of inflation, SNAP benefit reductions, and geopolitical uncertainty, which has pressured category growth, particularly in cereal, snacks, and dog feeding. In addition, sales mix has been negatively impacted by more consumers buying items on promotion, though the company did state that the depth and frequency of its promotions are largely in line with a year ago. We are disappointed with the revised outlook, as we had hoped to see a potential inflection in the back half of this fiscal year, driven by recent price investments and more effective innovation. We cut our 12-month target to $47 from $55, about 13x our FY 27 EPS of $3.65 (cut from $3.95; FY 26 cut to $3.51 from $3.72).

MT Newswires does not provide investment advice. Unauthorized reproduction is strictly prohibited.
